solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The injected Rust test explicitly constructs RunCommand::PythonRemote(tf, vec![...]) where tf is a tempfile::NamedTempFile, and verify_as_command_arity calls c.as_command(i) with exactly two args. An alternative fix that, say, stores a PathBuf instead of NamedTempFile, or has as_command take an additional context parameter, would fail these tests despite being a correct fix. The tests are locked to the gold solution's specific type choices.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core f2p tests inject Rust code that must compile and run (behavioral_result fixture uses cargo test), and other tests run cargo check/cargo fmt. While some tests do text inspection of signatures, the key behavioral tests actually compile and execute Rust code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (test_no_unwrap_on_downloaded_script, test_run_fn_no_downloaded_script_param, test_lib_no_downloaded_script_threading) cannot be satisfied by stubs — they require actual compilation of injected Rust code with specific type signatures, and signature checks that 'downloaded_script' is absent. A no-op change would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral requirements listed in instruction.md are tested: no downloaded_script param in run(), no Option param in as_command(), no downloaded_script in run_project(), PythonRemote embeds a file type, and no .unwrap() on downloaded script.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized into two clearly labeled sections (SECTION 1 without behavioral fixture, SECTION 2 with it), each test has descriptive names and docstrings, and tests are tagged with [repo_tests], [pr_diff], [static] markers.
